originalTitle: Meta won’t have to face the next planned social media addiction trial
summary: Less than a week before Meta's lawyers were set to return to a Los Angeles courtroom, the plaintiff accusing the platform of inflicting harm dropped the case. Brought by 15-year-old Florida plaintiff going by initials R.K.C., the case was set to be the second in a set of bellwether trials meant to test legal arguments […]
content: Less than a week before Meta’s lawyers were set to return to a Los Angeles courtroom, the plaintiff accusing the platform of inflicting harm dropped the case. Brought by 15-year-old Florida plaintiff going by initials R.K.C., the case was set to be the second in a set of bellwether trials meant to test legal arguments that social media giants allegedly broke the law by creating features that hooked and harmed teens. TikTok, Snap, and YouTube previously settled claims brought by R.K.C. for undisclosed amounts. “In light of the overall successful result of the litigation and his concerns about enduring a grueling weekslong trial, he has elected to withdraw his claims against Meta,” R.K.C.’s lawyers Emily Jeffcott and Rahul Ravipudi said in a statement. “He’s ready to close this chapter and focus on his recovery and engage in therapy as he aspires to have a normal life.” That means Meta got out of this case without having to pay a settlement. “The claims never held up, and this outcome makes clear that we will not back away from defending ourselves against baseless lawsuits,” Meta spokesperson Andy Stone said in a statement.  Multiple cases have already begun to test claims about social media companies allegedly misleading users about the safety of their products and inflicting harm. Meta and Google’s YouTube lost the first bellwether case tried at the courthouse, with a jury finding them to be negligent and ordering them to pay a total of $6 million in compensatory and punitive damages to the single plaintiff. Separately, a New Mexico jury ordered the company to pay a $375 million penalty, and the state is preparing for the next phase of trial seeking structural business changes. There’s still seven more bellwether cases on deck in the California state courthouse in LA, and a separate set of cases pending in a federal court in Oakland. Meta, Tiktok, Snap, and YouTube settled with a school district that was set to be the first in that separate set of bellwether cases seeking money to cover mental health-related costs to students allegedly caused by their platforms. Meta is still set to face claims brought by state attorneys general in federal court next month, alleging that it illegally misled the public about harmful and allegedly addictive features.
chineseTitle: Meta 将不必面临下一次计划中的社交媒体成瘾试验
chineseSummary: 距离 Meta 的律师返回洛杉矶法庭不到一周，指控该平台造成伤害的原告撤销了诉讼。该案由 15 岁的佛罗里达州原告提起，名字缩写为 R.K.C.，本案将是一系列旨在测试法律论证的风向标审判中的第二起案件 […]
chineseContent: 距离 Meta 的律师返回洛杉矶法庭不到一周，指控该平台造成伤害的原告撤回了诉讼。该案由 15 岁的佛罗里达州原告提起，名字缩写为 R.K.C.，它是一系列领先审判中的第二起案件，旨在检验社交媒体巨头涉嫌通过创建吸引和伤害青少年的功能而违法的法律论点。 TikTok、Snap 和 YouTube 此前已就 R.K.C. 提起的索赔达成和解。金额未公开。 R.K.C. 的律师艾米丽·杰科特 (Emily Jeffcott) 和拉胡尔·拉维普迪 (Rahul Ravipudi) 在一份声明中表示：“鉴于诉讼的整体成功结果以及他对忍受长达数周的艰苦审判的担忧，他选择撤回对 Meta 的索赔。” “他已经准备好结束这一章，专注于康复并接受治疗，因为他渴望过上正常的生活。”这意味着 Meta 在无需支付和解金的情况下就结案了。 Meta 发言人安迪·斯通 (Andy Stone) 在一份声明中表示：“这些指控从未成立，这一结果清楚地表明，我们不会放弃为自己辩护，以应对毫无根据的诉讼。”  多个案件已经开始检验有关社交媒体公司涉嫌误导用户产品安全性并造成伤害的说法。 Meta 和谷歌旗下的 YouTube 在法院审理的第一起领头羊案件中败诉，陪审团认定他们存在疏忽，并命令他们向单一原告支付总计 600 万美元的补偿性和惩罚性赔偿。另外，新墨西哥州陪审团命令该公司支付 3.75 亿美元的罚款，该州正在准备下一阶段的审判，寻求结构性业务变革。加利福尼亚州法院还有另外七起领头羊案件正在审理中
whyItMatters: 这条信息属于 AI 与科技趋势信号，适合用于观察工具选型、产品机会、开发者关注点和技术路线变化。
